title: Mapping Workflow
text: This page documents the unified mapping workflow (STAR, bwa, bowtie2) driven via ./scripts/bcfngs.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Overview

One workflow file: workflow/mapping.smk
One CLI entrypoint:

./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run

Default mapper: star
Default read mode: paired-end
Default bigWig conversion: bamCoverage --normalizeUsing CPM --binSize 10

Output layout:
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/<mapper>/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/<mapper>/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/<mapper>/bigwig/<sample>.bw
For STAR, the workflow also keeps:

<sample>.counts.tab
<sample>.SJ.out.tab

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Required configuration
Update configs/run.yaml:
project: "P000"
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/"
outdir: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping"
organism: "Caenorhabditis_elegans"
threads: 16
ram_bytes: 168632718037
organism must be a single organism key from configs/genomes.yaml.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
FASTQ pattern auto-detection
The workflow detects input naming in this fixed order:

*_R1.fastq.gz
*_R1_001.fastq.gz
*_L1_R1.fastq.gz
*.conc.R1.fastq.gz

In paired-end mode, the corresponding R2 file must exist.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Basic usage
Dry-run first:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
Run:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run
Select mapper(s):
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --mapper star,bwa,bowtie2
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --mapper bwa
Read mode:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --single-end
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --paired-end
Override config values for a single run:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --project P152 \
  --organism Drosophila_melanogaster \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P152/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
Keep intermediary files (default is cleanup):
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --keep-intermediary-files
Tune bigWig options:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --bw-normalization CPM --bw-bin-size 10

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
SLURM execution
Run mapping on SLURM:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --executor slurm --jobs 20
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --executor slurm --jobs 20
Defaults:

Partition: p.hpcl8
Mail user: yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de
Mail type: ALL
Time: 04:00:00

Key SLURM overrides:
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --executor slurm \
  --slurm-partition p.hpcl8 \
  --slurm-time 04:00:00 \
  --slurm-cpus-per-task 20 \
  --slurm-star-mem-mb 64000 \
  --slurm-aligner-mem-mb 32000
The wrapper sets per-rule resources so lightweight rules (indexing and bamCoverage) do not request mapper-level memory.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Additional Examples Moved From snakemake.Rmd
These command blocks were moved here to keep mapping examples in one place.
# default (paired-end, STAR)
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run

# one mapper
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper bwa

# all mappers
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper star,bwa,bowtie2
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --project P152 \
  --organism Drosophila_melanogaster \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P152/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --executor slurm --jobs 20
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --executor slurm --jobs 20
Legacy STAR-only direct Snakemake examples:
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/

# for single-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.SingleEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
# for paired-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
Flag meanings for the legacy command style:

-n: dry-run
-p: print shell commands
-s: snakefile path
-j: number of cores/jobs

title: HOWTO
text: This is a short internal HOW-TO for the bcfngs mapping automation.

title: HOWTO
text:
1) Prepare environment
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check
<<<<<<< Updated upstream 4. run the command snakemake -nps archive/legacy/getGenome_IndexGenome.Snakefile to check that the new genome is added to the workflow (the -n is a dry-run which just output the command without performing the tasks.) 5. run the same command without the -n - snakemake -ps archive/legacy/getGenome_IndexGenome.Snakefile -j 12. This will download the fastA and gtf files from the given links and create the index files for them as well. The parameter -j sets the number pf cores used in the snakemake run. If the number of cores is smaller then the one sets for e.g. STAR, only this numner of cores will be used.

Mapping
Mapping will be done with the STAR aligner for a specific genome either in a paired-end or single-end format.

title: snakemake
text:
snakemake is a workflow engine for reproducible data analysis pipelines.
At a high level, you define analysis steps as rules with inputs/outputs, and Snakemake:

resolves dependencies automatically,
runs only what is missing or outdated,
supports dry-runs and lint checks,
scales from local execution to cluster schedulers.

This makes it useful for routine, repeatable analyses where the same logic is applied to many projects.

title: snakemake
text:
snakemake is a workflow engine for reproducible data analysis pipelines.
At a high level, you define analysis steps as rules with inputs/outputs, and Snakemake:

resolves dependencies automatically,
runs only what is missing or outdated,
supports dry-runs and lint checks,
scales from local execution to cluster schedulers.

This makes it useful for routine, repeatable analyses where the same logic is applied to many projects.

title: snakemake
text:
How this project uses Snakemake
This repository has two main Snakemake workflows:

Genome source download + index building:

workflow/genomes.smk
config: configs/genomes.yaml

Sample mapping and coverage generation:

workflow/mapping.smk
config: configs/run.yaml


For most users, the recommended entrypoint is the wrapper:
./scripts/bcfngs
It adds preflight checks and standard defaults around Snakemake execution.

title: snakemake
text:
Core Snakemake commands (general examples)
Lint a workflow:
snakemake --lint -s workflow/genomes.smk
Preview what would run (dry-run + print commands + reason summary):
snakemake -npr -s workflow/genomes.smk --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
Run locally with a fixed number of cores:
snakemake -pr -j 20 -s workflow/genomes.smk --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
Dry-run mapping workflow with current run configuration:
snakemake -npr -s workflow/mapping.smk --configfile configs/run.yaml

title: snakemake
section: Typical ways to use it in this project
text:
Typical ways to use it in this project
Use bcfngs for day-to-day operations:
# environment
./scripts/bcfngs env check

# genomes
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20

# mapping
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run
Use direct Snakemake commands when you want lower-level control, debugging, or development work.

title: snakemake
text:
Where to find task-specific instructions
This page is the Snakemake overview.
For detailed procedures, use:

Environment setup
Updating genomes and indices
Mapping workflow

title: Home
text: This website documents the BCF NGS mapping automation workflow. It is designed for reproducible, fast, and consistent preliminary analysis of sequencing projects at the core facility.

title: Home
text:
Why this tool exists
The same mapping tasks are repeated across many projects: environment setup, genome preparation, and standardized alignment/output generation. This tool provides one consistent interface so these steps are less error-prone, easier to review, and easier to run on local machines or HPC.

title: Home
text:
What the workflow can do

Build and maintain a dedicated micromamba environment for the pipeline.
Check whether a newer Ensembl release is available.
Download FASTA/GTF files and build versioned genome indices.
Build indices for multiple aligners (STAR, bwa, bowtie2).
Run preflight validation (lint, config checks, dry-run) before real execution.
Map single-end or paired-end FASTQ data to a selected organism.
Produce mapping outputs and normalized bigWig tracks for visualization.
Run with local execution or SLURM execution.

title: Home
text:
Main entry point: scripts/bcfngs
The main command-line wrapper is bcfngs (scripts/bcfngs). It is the recommended way to run the workflow end-to-end because it centralizes checks, planning, and execution.
Available command groups:

bcfngs env ... for environment management.
bcfngs genomes ... for genome download/index lifecycle.
bcfngs mapping ... for project mapping runs.

title: Home
text:
Available steps in bcfngs

Environment setup and validation

bcfngs env create
bcfngs env update
bcfngs env check

Genome maintenance

bcfngs genomes check-updates
bcfngs genomes plan
bcfngs genomes build

Mapping workflow

bcfngs mapping plan (dry-run + checks)
bcfngs mapping run (real run)

title: Home
text:
Short usage summary
# 1) create/update and validate environment
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check

# 2) (optional) check for newer Ensembl release
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates

# 3) plan and build genomes/indices
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build

# 4) plan and run mapping
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --paired-end --mapper star
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --paired-end --mapper star
Configuration is split into:

configs/genomes.yaml for organisms, releases, and index settings.
configs/run.yaml for per-project mapping settings (project, input path, organism, output path).

title: Home
text:
Detailed guides
This page is an overview. Full details are documented in the dedicated sections:

Environment setup
Snakemake workflow details
Updating genomes
Legacy quick HOWTO

title: Updating genomes and indices
text: This page collects the internal procedures for:

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
When to update genomes / indices
Once every few years the genomic sequences must be updated. Also the STAR version for mapping progresses and should be updated when needed.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
Prerequisites / where to run
You can use any server as long as you can run snakemake and the required tools. The recommended method is micromamba (see environment). Default environment path used on the cluster: /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
How-to (by scenario)

First time setup (recommended)
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check


Routine: re-run indexing (no config changes)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20

# optional explicit preflight (or use --no-skip-plan on build)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan


Only rebuild one or two organisms
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus


Ensembl published a new release

Check:

./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates

If an update is available, bump releases.ensembl in configs/genomes.yaml.
Then:

./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan


You updated STAR/bwa/bowtie2 (rebuild indices into new versioned folders)

Update tools:

./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check

Update index_versions in configs/genomes.yaml (these strings are used in output folder names).
Rebuild:

./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20


HPC modules fallback (aligners from modules)
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan


Recommended (micromamba)
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check


HPC modules fallback (optional)
You can use modules for vendor tools:
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa

title: Updating genomes and indices
section: 1) Check for a new Ensembl release (report only)
text:
1) Check for a new Ensembl release (report only)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
2) Update genome sources (if needed)
Genome sources are configured in configs/genomes.yaml. Organism identifiers use scientific names (e.g. Homo_sapiens, Mus_musculus).
To add or update a genome:

Add/update the organism entry under organisms:.
For Ensembl sources, keep releases.ensembl pinned (default: 116). Bumping the release is a controlled change.
Set sa_index appropriately for STAR indexing.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
3) Build genomes + indices (safe workflow)
Build directly (default behavior skips preflight plan checks):
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
Force preflight checks + lint + dry-run before build:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan
Run through SLURM instead of local execution:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --executor slurm --jobs 20

# if SLURM executor is unavailable, refresh the environment first
./scripts/bcfngs env update
To build only a subset:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
4) Versioned indices + symlinks
Indices are built into versioned directories (e.g. starIndex-<version>) and then symlinked so mapping can always use stable paths:

/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/starIndex
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bwaIndex
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bowtie2Index

Symlinks are updated automatically by the Snakemake workflow after successful index creation.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
5) Mapping (per project)
Mapping is now handled by a unified workflow (workflow/mapping.smk) and wrapper commands:

./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run

Supported mappers are STAR, bwa, and bowtie2 (selectable by --mapper).

Change parameters before mapping
Adjust configs/run.yaml:
project: "P152"
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P152/conc.fastq/"
outdir: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping"
organism: "Drosophila_melanogaster"
threads: 16


Run mapping (dry-run first)
# default mapper and mode (STAR, paired-end)
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run

# choose mapper(s)
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper star,bwa,bowtie2

# single-end mode
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --single-end
For more usage details, see mapping.qmd.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
Additional Examples Moved From snakemake.Rmd
These command blocks were moved here to keep topic-specific examples in one place.
# genomes
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20

# optional explicit preflight (or use --no-skip-plan on build)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan

# run jobs through SLURM (instead of local execution)
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --executor slurm --jobs 20

# if SLURM executor is reported as unavailable, refresh the env first
./scripts/bcfngs env update

# override defaults for a specific cluster/queue when needed
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --executor slurm --jobs 20 --slurm-partition <other_partition>

# SLURM defaults used automatically in slurm mode:
# --partition p.hpcl8
# --mail-user yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de
# --mail-type ALL
# --time 04:00:00 (240 min)
# --cpus-per-task 20
# --mem-per-cpu 20G

# check if a newer Ensembl release exists
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates
# all organisms
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20

# subset
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
This page focuses on the workflow environment itself: what it contains, how bcfngs env manages it, and practical command examples.
The environment is defined in env/environment.yml and is managed through:

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
What bcfngs env controls
bcfngs env wraps micromamba and standardizes environment setup across users and servers.
It does three things:

create: creates a new environment from env/environment.yml
update: updates an existing environment from env/environment.yml
check: validates required tools and prints detected versions as JSON

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
Command syntax and options
./scripts/bcfngs [--profile micromamba|modules] [--env-prefix PATH] env <create|update|check>

--profile

micromamba (default): run tools from the micromamba environment
modules: load HPC modules first, then run checks in the selected env

Note: env create and env update require --profile micromamba.
--profile modules is intended for env check.


--env-prefix
Environment path prefix. If omitted, bcfngs uses its built-in default path.
Common choices:

project-local: .mamba/env
shared cluster path: ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
Installing micromamba
Install micromamba from the micromamba releases.
"${SHELL}" <(curl -L https://micro.mamba.pm/install.sh)

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
section: Examples for all bcfngs env options
text:
Examples for all bcfngs env options

1) env create
Create at the default environment prefix:
./scripts/bcfngs env create
Create at a custom path:
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create
Create explicitly with micromamba profile (equivalent to default profile):
./scripts/bcfngs --profile micromamba --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create


2) env update
Update environment at the default prefix:
./scripts/bcfngs env update
Update a custom environment path:
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env update
Update explicitly with micromamba profile:
./scripts/bcfngs --profile micromamba --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env update


3) env check
Check default environment:
./scripts/bcfngs env check
Check a custom environment:
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check
Check with HPC modules profile:
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check
Example output:
{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.11b",
  "bwa": "...",
  "bowtie2": "...",
  "samtools": "samtools 1.23"
}

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
Common workflows

First-time setup (recommended)
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check


Routine update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check


Run a one-off command without activating
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes snakemake --help
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes STAR --version

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
Notes and limitations

--profile modules does not support env create or env update.
env check verifies the core toolchain (python, snakemake, STAR, bwa, bowtie2-build, samtools, curl).
If you need reproducibility snapshots, export explicit package lists after updates:

mkdir -p env/locks
micromamba list -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --explicit > env/locks/explicit-YYYY-MM-DD.txt

title: Environment (micromamba + bcfngs)
text:
Additional Examples Moved From snakemake.Rmd
# create/update env (only once, then as needed)
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env create
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check

# refresh env and re-check (for example after tool/plugin updates)
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes env check

title: conda
text:
This page explains how to create and maintain the workflow environment using conda commands.
The repository environment is defined in:

env/environment.yml

It includes snakemake, aligners (STAR, bwa, bowtie2), and supporting tools (samtools, bedtools, curl, wget, etc.).
To learn more about conda, see the official documentation.

title: conda
text:
This page explains how to create and maintain the workflow environment using conda commands.
The repository environment is defined in:

env/environment.yml

It includes snakemake, aligners (STAR, bwa, bowtie2), and supporting tools (samtools, bedtools, curl, wget, etc.).
To learn more about conda, see the official documentation.

title: conda
text:
1) Create the environment
You can create it either by name or by path prefix.
By name:
conda env create --name bcfngs-mapping --file env/environment.yml
By prefix (useful on shared systems):
conda env create --prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --file env/environment.yml

title: conda
text:
2) Activate the environment
By name:
conda activate bcfngs-mapping
By prefix:
conda activate ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
If activation fails in a fresh shell, initialize conda once:
conda init
Then open a new terminal and activate again.

title: conda
text:
3) Verify the installation
python --version
snakemake --version
STAR --version
bowtie2-build --version
samtools --version

title: conda
text:
4) Update the environment after changes
When env/environment.yml is updated, run:
conda env update --name bcfngs-mapping --file env/environment.yml --prune
Or (prefix-based):
conda env update --prefix ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --file env/environment.yml --prune
--prune removes packages that are no longer listed in the file.

title: conda
text:
5) Optional: export explicit package lists
For reproducibility snapshots:
mkdir -p env/locks
conda list --explicit --name bcfngs-mapping > env/locks/explicit-conda-YYYY-MM-DD.txt

title: conda
text:
Notes

Create once, then activate on each new login/session.
Keep env/environment.yml as the source of truth.
In this repository, ./scripts/bcfngs env ... is implemented with micromamba. This page provides equivalent conda-native commands.
